title: anisomeles viscidula
intro: Anisomeles viscidula, also known as the sticky anisomeles or viscid anisomeles, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to tropical Asia, and its leaves, stems, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. Anisomeles viscidula is said to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate health issues such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory problems, wounds, and other ailments.
